interactions.summary: Jiang tersely confirms that Netanyahu's side understood the war logic in advance, and the host asks him to repeat the point for emphasis.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang answer and host clarification prompt

interactions.summary: The host tries to close by thanking Jiang and hoping for intervention, while Jiang responds with warm praise for the show.
The host admits escalating anxiety and asks why Wall Street still appears calm if catastrophe is so near.
Jiang answers that elite actors welcome blood-soaked dislocation because crisis is when they can buy cheaply and consolidate wealth.
The host follows up by asking whether the same actors will treat a market crash as a buying opportunity.

claims.claim: Jiang says Netanyahu and the Zionist side planned the present war trajectory in advance rather than stumbling into it blindly.
Jiang says financial elites thrive on violent breakdown because social panic creates the cheapest conditions for private fortune-making.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang turns market panic into predatory opportunity for the people already positioned to own the aftermath.
signature_moments.source_phrase: When there's blood on the streets, that's the best time to buy.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It compresses his moral and financial reading of elite behavior into one memorable line.

chronology_notes.note: This packet moves from Jiang's brief confirmation that the war was planned to his explanation of why market elites still look calm: they expect to profit from the destruction.

uncertainty_notes.note: seg-0132 contains overlapping farewell chatter, but the broad function is clear: the host is trying to close while expressing gratitude and residual hope.
